title: Infrastructure and Stewardship
subtitle: Confirmed assets, current risks, and the case for stronger stewardship structure
summary: Sutton SignWriting has real infrastructure assets, active stewardship, and continuity risks that justify a stronger accountable structure.
synopsis: Sutton SignWriting is existing infrastructure with real assets, active stewardship, and identifiable continuity risks that justify a stronger accountable stewardship structure.

slides.title: Existing Infrastructure
slides.summary: Sutton SignWriting already includes domains, servers, code, data, fonts, standards work, documentation, and public language resources.

image_prompts.concrete: Infrastructure map slide showing domains, servers, software, fonts, data, standards, documentation, and public resources for SignWriting
image_prompts.abstract: A mature infrastructure graph with multiple maintained layers

slides.speech: This white paper begins by naming what already exists. Sutton SignWriting is not only a body of ideas. It includes domains, servers, code, fonts, data, standards-facing records, documentation, and public language resources.

slides.title: Operational Pressure
slides.summary: Real international use already creates maintenance, scaling, documentation, and continuity pressure.

image_prompts.concrete: Operational infrastructure slide showing public use, server pressure, maintenance work, documentation, and international partners
image_prompts.abstract: Real use creating visible operational load

slides.speech: The operational pressure is concrete. Public use creates load. Legacy systems need maintenance. Documentation and onboarding need attention. International work depends on continuity that cannot rest indefinitely on informal arrangements.

slides.title: Risk Profile
slides.summary: The main risks are practical: service interruptions, loss of knowledge, weak onboarding, and limited research reuse.

image_prompts.concrete: Risk profile slide listing service continuity, knowledge transfer, onboarding, research reuse, and standards follow-through
image_prompts.abstract: Continuity risks mapped to practical stewardship functions

bullets: Knowledge concentrated in too few places
slides.speech: The risk profile is practical. Without stronger stewardship, the likely problems are service interruptions, loss of tacit knowledge, weak onboarding, reduced research reuse, and limited standards follow-through.
slides.notes: Avoid crisis language; name practical risks.

slides.title: Host Function
slides.summary: A host structure would make responsibility, maintenance, research use, and succession planning more accountable.

image_prompts.concrete: Host structure slide showing accountability for maintenance, documentation, research reuse, standards, and succession planning
image_prompts.abstract: Stewardship functions becoming accountable under a host structure

slides.speech: A host structure would not need to invent SignWriting. Its value would be to make responsibility, maintenance, documentation, research use, standards follow-through, and succession planning more accountable.
